title: sideroxylon pomiferum
intro: Sideroxylon pomiferum, also known as the "Tree of Life" or "Kakadu plum," is a plant species native to northern Australia and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant's edible fruit has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, boasting medicinal properties such as high antioxidant and antibacterial activity, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The fruit of the Sideroxylon pomiferum is rich in vitamin C, with a single fruit containing up to 3,300 milligrams, making it a valuable resource for maintaining a healthy immune system and supporting skin health. Herbal preparations of the plant, including extracts and powders, are used to create supplements, which can be applied topically or taken orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
